analysis.consistency: The spell description is internally consistent regarding planar mechanics, movement costs, and sensory limitations while in the border region of the ethereal plane. The higher-level casting effect scales logically with slot level.
analysis.quality: The description is high quality, clearly detailing the complex mechanical effects of transitioning between planes (movement, interaction restrictions, reentry hazards). The limitation that it doesn't work on the ethereal plane itself is a good mechanical safeguard.

mechanics.interaction_rules: Only interact with creatures also on the ethereal border; ignores non-ethereal objects/effects.
mechanics.movement_cost: Up or down movement costs 1 extra foot per foot moved (effectively double cost for vertical movement).
mechanics.reentry_hazard: If shunted due to occupying the same space upon return, takes force damage equal to 2x the distance moved.
mechanics.scaling: At 8th level, targets 2 creatures (self + 1). At 9th level, targets 3 creatures (self + 2). The description states 'up to three willing creatures (including you) for each slot level above 7th,' implying +1 creature per slot level above 7th, which means 8th level = 2 targets, 9th level = 3 targets. This interpretation aligns with standard D&D 5e conventions for this type of scaling, although the phrasing could be slightly clearer (e.g., 'you can target one additional willing creature for each slot level above 7th').
mechanics.limitations: No effect if cast while already on the Ethereal Plane or a plane that does not border it.
